story_assessments.story_title: ChatGPT Work is being framed as a repeatable business system, not a general chatbot
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, small business operators, and ops-minded knowledge workers who live in spreadsheets, email, calendars, CRM and task trackers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want faster first drafts and cleaner recurring reviews without copying information between too many tools
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI says ChatGPT Work, with Codex built in, can move beyond answering questions and into work across web, mobile and desktop, with examples from Zapier, RingCentral, Virgin Atlantic and NVIDIA
story_assessments.stop_reason: This is not just a chatbot upgrade; it is a shift toward AI handling recurring business tasks across the tools people already use
story_assessments.promised_payoff: One AI layer can draft a weekly review, launch check or postmortem from source material already in work systems
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI says ChatGPT Work can move beyond answering questions and into work across web, mobile and desktop, and cites real workflow examples from several companies
search_phrases: use ChatGPT with CRM and spreadsheets
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong practical fit for people who manage repeatable work. The story is clear, searchable and easy to translate into a workflow example, though it is less visually striking than the Canva story.

story_assessments.story_title: Canva is bundling design, research, scheduling and sheets into one creative workflow
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, social media managers, small marketing teams, and brand operators
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want one place to move from idea to asset to schedule without app-hopping or losing brand consistency
story_assessments.interest_signal: Canva says Canva AI 2.0 adds connectors, scheduling, web research, brand intelligence, Sheets AI and Canva Code 2.0 on top of conversational design and agentic editing
story_assessments.stop_reason: This is a workflow stack story, not another isolated image tool announcement
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A single system can help build the brief, create the design, keep the brand consistent and prepare publishing steps
story_assessments.proof_point: Canva says Canva AI 2.0 is in research preview and connects drafting, research, brand controls and scheduling in one loop
story_assessments.score_reason: The strongest mix of practicality and visual clarity. It is easy to explain quickly, immediately useful to creators, and highly suited to short-form and visual platforms.

story_assessments.story_title: Notion is becoming the place where teams coordinate multiple agents
story_assessments.target_audience: Team leads, operations managers, and knowledge workers coordinating AI-assisted projects
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need a visible place to assign AI work, review output and keep the handoff attached to the project record
story_assessments.interest_signal: Notion says users can assign external agents such as Claude and Cursor from a shared board, while meeting notes get speaker labels and agents can handle more file types
story_assessments.stop_reason: The real change is coordination, not generation; teams need to track what the agent did and what happens next
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A shared board can act as the handoff layer for brief, draft, review and approved output
story_assessments.proof_point: Notion 3.6 adds external agents, speaker-labeled meeting notes and support for more file types including Excel and PowerPoint
story_assessments.score_reason: Useful and distinctive for teams, with a strong coordination angle. It is slightly less immediate than the Canva and ChatGPT stories, but it has solid operational relevance.

story_assessments.story_title: Enterprise AI is moving into regulated workflows, with humans still in the loop
story_assessments.target_audience: Enterprise leaders, operations teams, and service businesses working in approval-based environments
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want faster triage and analysis without removing the human approval step in sensitive workflows
story_assessments.interest_signal: Anthropic says UST is integrating Claude into engineering and client workflows across chip design, telecom, healthcare and banking, with recommended actions still routed to a person for approval in sensitive settings
story_assessments.stop_reason: This is the adoption pattern to watch: AI is being used where approval and review already matter
story_assessments.promised_payoff: AI can reduce manual scripting, speed triage and make human review more informed
story_assessments.proof_point: Anthropic says UST plans to train 20,000 associates on Claude and that sensitive workflows still keep human sign-off
story_assessments.score_reason: Good workplace relevance and strong discussion potential, but less visual and slightly less searchable than the consumer-facing workflow stories.

story_assessments.story_title: Google is making AI ads easier to identify
story_assessments.target_audience: Advertisers, marketers, content teams, and anyone managing paid creative
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need to know how provenance and disclosure fit into the ad production process
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google says it is adding a 'How this ad was made' panel across Search, YouTube and Discover, and advertisers must label AI-generated or AI-edited content using provided tools
story_assessments.stop_reason: Once AI touches creative, disclosure becomes part of production instead of a cleanup step
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Teams can treat provenance like a checklist item for publishing, buying and review
story_assessments.proof_point: Google is expanding AI transparency across Search, YouTube and Discover with disclosure tools for advertisers
story_assessments.score_reason: Highly relevant for marketers and platform users, with a clear operational consequence. It is strong for professional audiences and discussion, though less broadly appealing than the Canva story.

tiktok.story_title: Canva is bundling design, research, scheduling and sheets into one creative workflow
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, social media managers, and small marketing teams
tiktok.opening: If you run content for a small brand, the biggest AI shift is not image generation. It is Canva trying to connect research, design, brand rules and scheduling in one workflow.
tiktok.payoff: That means one place can help you build the brief, make the assets, keep the brand consistent, and prep the publishing plan.
tiktok.selection_reason: This story is visually easy to understand, clearly practical for creators, and has the strongest stop-and-watch value for a short video.

youtube.story_title: ChatGPT Work is being framed as a repeatable business system, not a general chatbot
youtube.target_audience: Small business owners, operators, and productivity-focused knowledge workers
youtube.opening: How can ChatGPT help with a weekly pipeline review instead of just answering prompts?
youtube.payoff: OpenAI says ChatGPT Work can move across web, mobile and desktop, and the useful move is to feed it source material from Sheets, CRM, email or Jira so it drafts the first pass of a recurring review.
youtube.selection_reason: This version answers a specific, searchable workflow question and gives a practical use case that is easy to understand in under a minute.

facebook.story_title: Enterprise AI is moving into regulated workflows, with humans still in the loop
facebook.target_audience: Managers, operations teams, and professionals in approval-heavy industries
facebook.opening: If your work lives inside approvals, the most useful AI is not full autonomy. It is AI that helps the review step move faster without removing the person who signs off.
facebook.payoff: Anthropic says UST is using Claude in engineering and client workflows, and that recommended actions still route to a person for approval in sensitive settings.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook performs well with recognizable workplace situations and practical discussion questions, and this story naturally invites people to compare how their own teams handle review.

pinterest.story_title: Canva is bundling design, research, scheduling and sheets into one creative workflow
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, marketers, and small business teams building content systems
pinterest.opening: AI Workflow Stack Checklist for Creators: Connect Design, Research, Scheduling and Brand Control
pinterest.payoff: A practical guide to using one tool for drafting, one for design, one for scheduling and one for review so a campaign can move from idea to published output with less app-hopping.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest rewards searchable how-to content and visual systems. This story already reads like a practical guide and is easy to package as a checklist.

linkedin.story_title: Google is making AI ads easier to identify
linkedin.target_audience: Marketers, ad operations teams, and workplace decision-makers
linkedin.opening: When AI touches ad creative, disclosure becomes part of production.
linkedin.payoff: Google says it is adding a 'How this ad was made' panel across Search, YouTube and Discover, and advertisers must label AI-generated or AI-edited content using its tools.
linkedin.selection_reason: LinkedIn is the best fit for an operational lesson about workflow, compliance, and provenance. It frames the change as a team process rather than a novelty update.

overall_reasoning: The research pack points to one clear theme: AI is becoming a connected workflow layer rather than a set of isolated features. Canva is the strongest visual and practical short-form story, so it leads TikTok and Pinterest. ChatGPT Work is the clearest searchable productivity question, so it fits YouTube Shorts. The enterprise approval story is the best discussion starter on Facebook. Google’s ad transparency update is the cleanest workplace consequence for LinkedIn because it turns provenance into an operational checklist item. All selections stay inside the verified facts and emphasize handoffs, review, scheduling, and disclosure rather than hype.
